SECTION_A.title: Pipeline Construction Steps
steps: 2. Vector similarity search and hybrid search
4. Generating Cypher queries from natural language questions

variants.name: Vector Similarity Search
variants.description: Basic vector-based retrieval using cosine similarity or Euclidean distance to find semantically similar text chunks.

variants.name: Hybrid Search
variants.description: Combines vector similarity search with full-text search for improved retrieval accuracy.

variants.name: Step-back Prompting
variants.description: Query rewriting technique that broadens specific queries to more general questions to improve vector retrieval accuracy.

variants.name: Parent Document Retriever
variants.description: Strategy that splits documents into smaller child chunks for embedding while returning the full parent document for context.

variants.name: Hypothetical Question Strategy
variants.description: Generates hypothetical questions that documents can answer and embeds those questions instead of the original text.

variants.name: Text2Cypher
variants.description: Generates Cypher queries from natural language questions to query graph databases directly.

variants.name: Agentic RAG
variants.description: System with multiple retrieval agents and a retriever router that selects the best retriever for each query.

variants.name: Global Search (Microsoft GraphRAG)
variants.description: Uses community summaries in a map-reduce approach to answer broad, thematic queries across the entire dataset.

variants.name: Local Search (Microsoft GraphRAG)
variants.description: Combines vector similarity search with graph traversal to answer entity-focused queries using connected entities, text chunks, and community summaries.

problems.description: LLMs are unaware of events or information not included in their training dataset.
problems.solution: RAG - Provide external knowledge base and retrieve relevant information at runtime.

problems.description: LLMs provide confident but incorrect or fabricated information.
problems.solution: Ground responses in retrieved factual information from trusted sources.

problems.description: LLMs cannot access proprietary or internal company information.
problems.solution: Use RAG with company-specific knowledge bases and maintain data privacy.

problems.description: Vector retrieval may pull information from different contracts, mixing relevant and irrelevant chunks.
problems.solution: Extract structured data from contracts and use knowledge graphs to maintain document boundaries.

problems.problem: Poor Retrieval Accuracy with Complex Queries
problems.description: Simple vector similarity search struggles with queries requiring filtering, aggregation, or complex relationships.
problems.solution: Use structured data extraction, text2cypher, and knowledge graphs for complex queries.

problems.description: Different representations of the same entity (e.g., 'UTI Asset Management Company' vs 'UTI Asset Management Company Ltd') create duplicates.
problems.solution: Implement entity resolution with domain-specific rules, string matching, and clustering algorithms.

problems.problem: Tool Selection Errors in Agentic RAG
problems.description: LLM may select wrong retrieval tool or fail to map user input to correct database values.
problems.solution: Use few-shot examples, clear tool descriptions, and implement answer critic for validation.

metrics.description: Smaller chunk sizes (600 tokens) extract more entity references than larger chunks (2400 tokens). More self-reflection iterations also increase entity extraction.
Sample benchmark evaluation showed: Answer Correctness: 0.7774, Context Recall: 0.7941, Faithfulness: 0.9657
Latency significantly lower for queries that don't require text2cypher, as avoiding additional LLM call speeds up response.

approaches.name: Continuous Query Updating
approaches.description: In agentic RAG systems, answers from previous questions can be used to rewrite and update subsequent questions with new information.

approaches.name: Entity and Relationship Summarization
approaches.description: MS GraphRAG merges multiple descriptions of the same entity or relationship using LLMs to generate concise summaries, ensuring key contextual details are preserved.

approaches.name: Community Detection Updates
approaches.description: As new entities and relationships are added to the graph, community detection algorithms can be rerun to update community structures and summaries.

approaches.name: Answer Critic Loop
approaches.description: In agentic RAG, if answers are incomplete or incorrect, the answer critic generates new questions to retrieve missing information and goes through another retrieval cycle.

approaches.name: Dynamic Tool Selection
approaches.description: Agentic RAG systems can dynamically select the most appropriate retrieval tool based on the query, allowing for adaptive and optimized retrieval strategies.
